examples.title: Basic Text File Write and Read
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental file input/output operations - creating a file, writing text to it, and reading back the content using ifstream and ofstream
examples.explanation:
1. Include <fstream> header for file operations
2. ofstream creates output file stream for writing
3. Check if file opened successfully using !outFile
4. Use << operator to write data to file
5. Always close file after operations
6. ifstream creates input file stream for reading
7. getline() reads file line by line into string

examples.title: File Appending and Status Checking
examples.description: Shows how to append data to existing files, check file existence, and handle multiple file operations with proper error handling
examples.explanation:
1. ios::app flag opens file in append mode - preserves existing content
2. stat() function checks if file exists before operations
3. Multiple file streams can be open simultaneously
4. getline() returns false at end of file
5. Line counter tracks number of entries
6. Proper file closing prevents resource leaks

examples.title: Binary File I/O with Structures and Random Access
examples.description: Advanced file operations using binary mode, storing structured data (records), and random access using seek operations for direct positioning
examples.explanation:
1. Binary mode (ios::binary) stores raw bytes without text formatting
2. Structures can be written directly using reinterpret_cast
3. sizeof(Student) gives fixed record size for random access
4. seekp() positions write pointer, seekg() positions read pointer
5. Offset calculation: record_number * sizeof(Record)
6. ios::beg sets position relative to file beginning
7. Random access allows direct access to any record without sequential reading
